2024-07-24.title: After Losing Crops to Drought, Sicily Fears Losing Tourism, Too
2024-07-24.abstract: Parts of southern Italy and other countries in the region are experiencing one of their worst droughts in decades. The authorities say they are working to at least save tourism.

2024-07-25.title: Most Valuable Player: A Star Raises the Profile of Women’s Soccer in Africa
2024-07-25.abstract: In seven years, Racheal Kundananji went from playing her first organized game to signing a multimillion-dollar contract. Her rise could have lasting effects.

2024-07-25.title: What a Professor’s Firing Shows About Sexual Harassment in China
2024-07-25.abstract: A top Chinese university described the conduct of a professor accused of sexual harassment as a moral failing, language feminists say downplays harm to women.

2024-07-25.title: U.K.’s Policy on Israel, Long Aligned With America’s, Veers Away
2024-07-25.abstract: Britain’s new government is likely to withdraw objections to the International Criminal Court prosecutor’s pursuit of a warrant for Benjamin Netanyahu, two people told The Times.

2024-07-25.title: Typhoon Gaemi Reaches China as Cargo Ship Sinks Off Taiwan
2024-07-25.abstract: The storm, weaker but still dangerous, made landfall just before 8 p.m. local time. Six sailors were still missing after a cargo ship sank near Taiwan.

2024-07-25.title: Fuel Tanker Sinks in Philippine Waters, Spilling Oil
2024-07-25.abstract: The coast guard rescued 16 of the vessel’s 17 crew members, but Typhoon Gaemi’s remnants were complicating efforts to contain the oil spill in Manila Bay.

2024-07-26.title: A Fake Trip and a Stunning Betrayal Landed Top Cartel Leaders in U.S. Hands
2024-07-26.abstract: Ismael Zambada García and Joaquín Guzmán López helped run the Sinaloa Cartel, one of the most dominant criminal groups in Mexico.

2024-07-26.title: Breaking Down Netanyahu’s Speech in Congress
2024-07-26.abstract: In his fourth speech to Congress, a record for a foreign leader, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel attempted to change the narrative about the fallout from the war in Gaza. Patrick Kingsley, the Jerusalem bureau chief for The New York Times, explains how Netanyahu used the speech to shift the focus to Iran and the threat it poses to Israel.

2024-07-26.title: Vintage of War
2024-07-26.abstract: A single image captures the change in fortunes for a wine cellar turned field hospital in Russian-occupied Ukraine.

2024-07-26.title: In Japan, Turning the Tables on Rude Customers
2024-07-26.abstract: The country is famous for its hospitality. But businesses have had enough of people screaming at workers or tormenting them on social media.

2024-07-26.title: Will These Sensational Skateboarding Tricks Win Japan Olympic Gold?
2024-07-26.abstract: They have landed some of the most difficult tricks in the history of skateboarding. But in Paris, they may have a problem.

2024-07-27.title: Snoop Dogg, NBC’s New Voice of the People
2024-07-27.abstract: The network hired the rapper for an expanded role on its broadcasts of the Summer Games in Paris after posting record-low viewership of the Tokyo competition.

2024-07-27.title: The French Swimmer Who Is Carrying His Country on His Back
2024-07-27.abstract: Léon Marchand, who has the potential to win medals in four individual events, bears much of the weight of France’s expectations. He is ready, he said, but added, “It does feel weird.”

2024-07-27.title: ‘No One to Talk To’: The Lingering Trauma of Russian Rape in Ukraine
2024-07-27.abstract: Prosecutors in Ukraine are pursuing cases of sexual assault by Russian soldiers, but survivors say more financial and psychological support is needed.

2024-07-27.title: Away From the War in Gaza, Another Palestinian Economy Is Wrecked
2024-07-27.abstract: With the closure of checkpoints, Israeli Arabs cannot come to Jenin and Tulkarm to shop, and West Bank Palestinians cannot leave to work in Israel, cutting incomes and building militancy.

2024-07-27.title: What to Know About Kamala Harris’s Foreign Policy Positions
2024-07-27.abstract: Here is what we know about the presumptive Democratic presidential nominee’s views on issues like migration and the wars in Ukraine and Gaza.

2024-07-28.title: The Move That May Take Sunisa Lee’s Name
2024-07-28.abstract: Sunisa Lee has been practicing a new difficult element in her uneven bars routine. If she completes it at the Olympics, it will be named “The Lee.”

2024-07-28.title: Echoes of the Fort McMurray Disaster as Fire Sweeps Into Jasper, Alberta
2024-07-28.abstract: About a third of the buildings in the mountain resort town have burned, raising questions about preparedness and the role of climate change.

2024-07-28.title: Fears of Escalation After Rocket From Lebanon Hits Soccer Field
2024-07-28.abstract: Diplomats were scrambling to prevent a surge in fighting, officials said, after Israel retaliated for the rocket fire that killed at least 12 children and teenagers in an Israeli-controlled town.

2024-07-28.title: Simone Biles Is Done Being Judged
2024-07-28.abstract: Three years after she dropped out of the Tokyo Games with a mental block, she is back at the Olympics with a defiant attitude — and a sense of where she is in the air.

2024-07-28.title: Russia Punches Through Weakened Lines in Eastern Ukraine
2024-07-28.abstract: Military analysts say Russian forces are increasingly investigating Ukrainian lines to identify weakened positions before attacking and breaking through.

2024-07-29.title: Venezuela’s Autocrat Is Declared Winner in Tainted Election
2024-07-29.abstract: The result, which would give Nicolás Maduro six more years as president, was disputed by the opposition, and the United States said it had “serious concerns.”

2024-07-29.title: Why Some Olympic Swimmers Think About Math in the Pool
2024-07-29.abstract: In a sport where gold and silver can be separated by a fraction of a second, many of the world’s top swimmers now scour data for even the smallest edge.

2024-07-29.title: Here’s What to Know About Venezuela’s Flawed Election
2024-07-29.abstract: Stark irregularities and suppression efforts could plunge the country back into instability and economic decline.

2024-07-29.title: Maduro Declared Winner in Flawed Venezuela Election
2024-07-29.abstract: Venezuela’s presidential election was riddled with irregularities and was disputed by the opposition.

2024-07-29.title: Stalkers, Disease and Doubt: A Gymnast’s Hard Road Back to the Games
2024-07-29.abstract: Sunisa Lee, the all-around women’s champion at the Tokyo Olympics, overcame personal strife, family expectations and serious illness to get to Paris.

2024-07-30.title: In the 1,500, There’s Katie Ledecky and Then There’s Everyone Else
2024-07-30.abstract: Ledecky has not lost a 1,500-meter freestyle race in 14 years. Even her rivals admit they hit the water knowing they’re racing for second.

2024-07-30.title: After Furious Battles, Ukraine Loses a Pair of Hard-Won Villages
2024-07-30.abstract: “It was like a fight between two packs of dogs,” said an officer, describing the struggle for one of the areas, Urozhaine. But “there came a moment when it made no sense to keep people there.”

2024-07-30.title: Olympic Men’s Triathlon Postponed Because of Pollution in the Seine
2024-07-30.abstract: Water-quality tests early Tuesday showed that the river running through Paris was unsafe for swimming, organizers said.

2024-07-30.title: Landslides in Southern India Kill More Than 100 People
2024-07-30.abstract: Rescue workers struggled to reach isolated villages in the state of Kerala, where torrential rains have washed away terrain. The death toll was expected to rise.

2024-07-30.title: Deadly Landslides Hit India
2024-07-30.abstract: The landslides left behind destruction in Kerala, where some people worked to clear a pile of uprooted trees as muddy waters flowed around a village.

2024-07-31.title: Their Parents Fled War. Now South Sudan’s Young Team Is in the Olympics.
2024-07-31.abstract: War, famine and floods have beleaguered the world’s youngest nation. The arrival of its men’s basketball team at the Paris Olympics is set to offer a modicum of hope.

2024-07-31.title: Israel Says It Killed Hezbollah Commander in Airstrike Near Beirut
2024-07-31.abstract: The strike was in retaliation for a deadly rocket attack this weekend in the Golan Heights. At least three civilians were killed and 74 others wounded on Tuesday, Lebanese officials said.

2024-07-31.title: Olympic Triathlons in Seine Will Go Ahead, Organizers Say
2024-07-31.abstract: The races were scheduled for Wednesday morning. The men’s event was postponed on Tuesday after tests showed the river was not safe for swimming.

2024-07-31.title: What We Know About the Assassination of Ismail Haniyeh
2024-07-31.abstract: The top Hamas leader, who was central to the group’s high-stakes negotiations and diplomacy, was killed in Iran’s capital.

2024-07-31.title: War Gives Palestinian Olympians a Wider Role: Athlete-Diplomats
2024-07-31.abstract: The Israel-Hamas war has given the eight Palestinians competing in Paris Olympics a higher profile than usual.
